title: vitex agnus-castus var. negundo
intro: Vitex agnus-castus var. negundo, also known as Chasteberry, is a plant species that belongs to the Lamiaceae family, native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. The medicinal properties of Vitex agnus-castus var. negundo include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and neuroprotective effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of this plant are said to include relief from menstrual cramps, menopausal symptoms, and anxiety, as well as potential anti-cancer and neuroprotective properties. Vitex agnus-castus var. negundo is traditionally used in herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and tinctures, which are often standardized to contain a specific concentration of the active compounds berberine and ursolic acid.
